body: @lolipopshock I set this up on my own repo but the tags where not created to trigger the agent actions on an issue.

comments.body:
@sepo-agent /answer I think this is a overall onboarding issue: 

1. when the user just set up the repo, we should perhaps try to post a first issue message showing sepo is set up. 
2. in that issue, we can 
    - tell the missing configurations: if the gh app is not installed , if the api key is configured, etc 
    - explaining next steps: how to use it (briefly), what are the customizations, etc. 
3. we can include a code block so users can directly copy and use it.

Agreed. I’d treat this as a first-run onboarding workflow, not only a label-creation fix.

Current relevant pieces:

- Mention entrypoint is already thin and routes through [`agent-entrypoint.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-entrypoint.yml).
- Label-trigger routing exists in [`agent-label.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-label.yml), but trigger labels like `agent/review` are documented, not proactively created.
- Status-label creation already has a helper path in [`add-label.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/add-label.ts), using `ensureLabel()` from [`github.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/github.ts).
- Auth readiness is centralized in [`resolve-github-auth`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/actions/resolve-github-auth/action.yml).
- Provider readiness is centralized in [`resolve-agent-provider`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/actions/resolve-agent-provider/action.yml).
- First-time memory setup already has a manual workflow in [`agent-memory-bootstrap.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-memory-bootstrap.yml).

Implementation approach I’d propose:

1. Add a new manual first-run workflow, probably `Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup`, that can be run after copying Sepo into a repo.
2. Reuse `resolve-github-auth` and `resolve-agent-provider` to detect:
   - whether GitHub auth resolves through app/OIDC/PAT/fallback token
   - whether `OPENAI_API_KEY` or `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` is available
   - whether memory/rubrics branches exist
   - whether expected trigger labels exist
3. Add a small typed CLI under `.agent/src/cli/`, for example `onboarding-check.ts`, to:
   - ensure built-in trigger labels exist: `agent/answer`, `agent/implement`, `agent/create-action`, `agent/review`, `agent/fix-pr`
   - create an onboarding issue if one does not already exist
   - post/update a checklist comment with the setup status and next steps
4. Keep the issue body practical and copyable, for example:

```md
@sepo-agent /answer Is Sepo configured correctly in this repository?

Try implementation after setup:

@sepo-agent /implement Create a small README update that verifies the agent can open a PR.

Try PR review on an open pull request:

@sepo-agent /review
```

5. Update docs:
   - [`README.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/README.md)
   - [`quick-start.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/overview/quick-start.md)
   - [`install-existing-repository.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/deployment/install-existing-repository.md)
   - [`supported-workflows.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md)

Execution plan:

1. Add shared constants for built-in labels so docs/tests/CLI do not drift.
2. Add `onboarding-check` CLI with tests using fake `gh`, following the existing `add-label` test pattern.
3. Add a new workflow_dispatch onboarding workflow that checks auth/provider readiness, creates labels, and opens/updates the setup issue.
4. Make the workflow non-destructive and idempotent: reruns should update the same onboarding issue instead of creating duplicates.
5. Document the new first-run step in quick start and install docs.
6. Run the `.agent` test suite and update generated dist if this repo expects built artifacts in CI.

I can implement this next if you approve this direction.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `implement` (round 2/10).

manual orchestrate start on issue; dispatching implement

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777531626680 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNTE1MDI3MjE6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6MjM6aW1wbGVtZW50OjIzOjI -->

**Sepo implementation finished**

- Branch: `agent/implement-issue-23/codex-25151531035`
- Pull request: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/62

Added an idempotent first-run onboarding workflow that resolves auth/provider readiness, builds the agent runtime, creates built-in agent trigger labels, and opens or updates a Sepo setup issue with status and copyable test commands. Updated setup docs and added CLI coverage; verified with `npm --prefix .agent test` and workflow YAML parsing.

title: Agent should create tags upon install/first run
